TCE_decay_chain.significance: TCE is a major detected contaminant showing increasing trend 1999-2008
TCE_decay_chain.forensic_implications: Presence of decay products indicates TCE contamination may be historically persistent or from multiple time periods

chlorinated_compounds.significance: Multiple chlorinated species suggesting multiple sources or historical industrial activities

1,2-DCA.interpretation: Possible mobilization event or new source activation

Chloroform.interpretation: Long-term background contamination, possibly from older spill

heavy_metals.co_occurrence_analysis: Heavy metals expected in boreholes near I-003 but TCE/chlorinated compound profile suggests separate source

PFAS_family.status: Not detected in TAHAL 2008 data (pre-PFAS awareness era)
PFAS_family.recommended_addition: PFAS should be added to monitoring parameters for Raanana from 2021 onwards

pattern_1.interpretation: TCE likely solvent for chemical reactions; chlorinated compounds byproducts

1999_2008_period.interpretation: Suggests ongoing release from identified sources or mobilization of aged contamination

2004_decrease.observation: Temporary drop from 18 to 0.9 ppb then return to 18 ppb in 2006
2004_decrease.hypothesis: Sampling artifact, analytical error, or transient flow variation

2006_spike.hypothesis: New source activation or mobilization event

forensics_analysis.recommendation_for_phase_3: Integrate flow direction with forensic analysis to predict future plume migration and identify potential remediation strategies

source_document: TAHAL 2008 Part B Table 10.6 and Part B pages 53-67
extraction_notes: Forensic interpretation based on documented contamination patterns and industry types; expert hydrogeologist review recommended
